name: Strawberry-Glazed Cream Cheese Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly butter a 9 or 10-inch springform pan.
2. For the crust, combine walnuts, graham crackers, and melted butter. Press compactly onto the bottom of the pan.
3. For the filling, beat cream cheese until smooth in a large bowl. Add eggs, sugar, lemon juice, and vanilla, then beat thoroughly. Pour over the crust.
4. Place the pan on a baking sheet to catch any dripping butter. Bake for 40-45 minutes (9-inch cake) or 50-55 minutes (10-inch cake). The cake may crack slightly but will settle and be covered by topping.
5. Let the cake stand at room temperature for 15 minutes, then keep the oven at 350°F (175°C).
6. For the topping, combine sour cream, sugar, and vanilla in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate.
7. When the cake is done baking, spoon the topping over it, starting at the center and extending to within 1/2 inch of the edge. Return to the oven for an additional 5 minutes.
8. Let cool, then refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 24 hours, or preferably 2-3 days.
9. For the glaze, several hours before serving, wash and hull strawberries and dry completely on paper towels. In a saucepan, combine a little jelly with cornstarch and mix well. Add the remaining jelly, Cointreau, and water, then cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until thickened and clear (about 5 minutes). Cool to lukewarm.
10. Loosen the cake from the pan and remove the springform. Arrange strawberries pointed end up over the top of the cake. Spoon glaze over the berries, allowing some to drip down the sides of the cake. Return to the refrigerator until the glaze is set.
